evals.query: I have a Kora HTTP server. Expose my OpenAPI spec at /api/openapi and an interactive Swagger UI at /api/docs.
expected_behavior: Adds the ru.tinkoff.kora:openapi-management dependency and keeps ru.tinkoff.kora:http-server-undertow
Adds OpenApiManagementModule to the @KoraApp interface's extends list
Places the spec under src/main/resources/openapi/ and lists it in openapi.management.file
Sets openapi.management.enabled = true, endpoint = "/api/openapi", swaggerui.enabled = true and swaggerui.endpoint = "/api/docs"
Notes that openapi.management toggles default to false so each must be set explicitly
Does not pull in an external documentation library or use field injection; uses constructor injection and OpenApiManagementModule only

evals.query: I added openapi-management and OpenApiManagementModule but /swagger-ui returns 404. What's wrong?
expected_behavior: Explains that openapi.management.enabled and swaggerui.enabled both default to false
Instructs to set openapi.management.enabled = true and swaggerui.enabled = true
Confirms swaggerui.endpoint defaults to /swagger-ui and the spec must be listed in openapi.management.file
Does not claim the toggles default to true

evals.query: Publish both api-v1.yaml and api-v2.yaml so Swagger UI lets me switch between the two versions.
expected_behavior: Lists both files in openapi.management.file = ["openapi/api-v1.yaml", "openapi/api-v2.yaml"]
Explains that with multiple files each spec is served at endpoint/{name} (filename without directory or extension), e.g. /openapi/api-v1
Enables swaggerui so the UI provides a spec selector
Does not suggest one GenerateTask call per viewer or any external (non-Kora) documentation tooling

evals.query: Keep the raw OpenAPI document reachable for tooling but hide the interactive Swagger UI in production.
expected_behavior: Keeps openapi.management.enabled = true so the raw spec stays served
Sets openapi.management.swaggerui.enabled = false in a production profile/override config
Optionally drives the toggle via an environment variable substitution like ${SWAGGER_UI_ENABLED:false}
Does not rely on a profile annotation; toggles documentation purely through HOCON config under openapi.management

evals.query: I want to keep Swagger UI enabled in production but require an Authorization header to reach the docs endpoints in my Kora app.
expected_behavior: Implements an HttpServerInterceptor whose intercept(Context, HttpServerRequest, InterceptChain) checks request.path() for the docs prefixes
Registers it as a @Component annotated with @Tag(HttpServerModule.class) so it runs globally without manual wiring
Returns HttpServerResponse.of(401, HttpBody.plaintext(...)) for unauthorized requests and calls chain.process(context, request) otherwise
Reads the Authorization header via request.headers().getFirst("Authorization")
Does not misuse HttpServerRequestHandler as an interceptor and does not invent a servlet-style filter; uses the Kora HttpServerInterceptor contract

evals.query: My OpenAPI spec uses oneOf with a discriminator for polymorphic types. Which built-in viewer should I enable, Swagger UI or RapiDoc?
expected_behavior: Recommends Swagger UI (swaggerui.enabled = true) for full discriminator and oneOf/anyOf support
Notes RapiDoc has limited support for discriminators and complex polymorphic schemas
Configures the choice under openapi.management (rapidoc.enabled left false)
Does not recommend an external (non-Kora) documentation viewer

evals.query: I generate my Kora server from user-http-server.yaml with the kora generator. How do I also publish that exact spec and Swagger UI?
expected_behavior: Adds openapi-management alongside the existing org.openapi.generator codegen and OpenApiManagementModule on the graph
Points openapi.management.file at the same src/main/resources/openapi/user-http-server.yaml the GenerateTask uses as inputSpec
Explains the spec stays on the classpath so no copy task is needed and docs cannot drift from generated code
Enables openapi.management.enabled and swaggerui.enabled, exposing /openapi and /swagger-ui
Does not hand-write a controller to serve the spec; relies on OpenApiManagementModule
